questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which year will have the same calendar as that of 2012?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the given years will have the same calendar as that of year 2003?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If 15th August, 2011 was Monday, what day of the week was on 17th September, 2011?

solutions.solution: Since, 2012 is a leap year ∴ 2012 + 28 = 2040 So, year 2040 will have the same calendar a that of 2012. (as we know that, a leap year repeats itself after 28 yr)

To have the same calendar as that year 2003, we must have 0 odd days between 2003 and the required year. Total odd days = 1 + 2 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 2 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 2 + 1 = 14 odd days = 2 complete weeks or 0 odd day Therefore, calendar for the year 2014 will be same as that of year 2003.

Here, total days in August, 2011 = 31 – 15 = 16 and total days in September, 2011 (1st to 17th) = 17 Total days = 33 ∴ 33 ÷ 7 = 7) 33 (4 28 5 33 days = (4 × 7 + 5) days = 4 weeks + 5 odd days ∴ Number of odd days = 5 Required day = Monday + 5 = Saturday
